I INTRODUCTION 
 
I.1 Product name  
OMI_CIRCULATION_BOUNDARY_BLKSEA_rim_current_index 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
The rim current is the major cyclonic gyre that encompasses the Black Sea 
basin. The Black Sea rim current intensity index (BSRCI) indicator provides a 
measure of the strength of the rim current in a specific year, in comparison to a 
multi-annual mean. 
 
The location of the rim current is defined as the area of the Black Sea with a 
depth between 200m and 1800m. Within the rim current, the indicator 
considers three distinct areas: 
- The northern area, between 33°E and 39°E; 
- The southern area, between 31.5°E and 35°E; 
- The combination of both northern and southern areas. 
The areas are shown overlaid on the bathymetry in Fig. 1. 
 
The product BLKSEA_MULTIYEAR_PHY_007_004 is used to calcul ate the daily 
mean total velocity 𝑉 from its Cartesian components for each region. The total 
velocity is then used to calculate the rim current intensity index as: 
𝐵𝑆𝑅𝐶𝐼 = 𝑉̅𝑎𝑛𝑛 − 𝑉̅𝑐𝑙
𝑉̅𝑐𝑙
 
With 𝑉̅𝑎𝑛𝑛 denoting the mean value of a particular year  and 𝑉̅𝑐𝑙 the multi -
annual/climatological mean over the period 1993-2020.  
The index constructed in this way will be close to 0 for a  annual mean state 
close to “normal”, while a positive (negative) value indicates a year with a 
more (less) intense rim current.
